content_of_post_to_comment:
I wanna get better on my real activities, get rid of endless planning and re-planning and never get my hands dirty doing it and maintain it.

I've already got some things running (gym, house cleaning etc) but not with the consistency that I want. So, facing this scenario, I want to get better disciplined on this, and start, develop myself to do more. Example of activities that I want to get more and better: Reading (to understand and learn), writing (small projects, like a hobby, but that actually improve the readers), connecting and motivating people on projects (small to big, aiming at community improvement).

I think that's a lot, but, I need to have something to strive for. And I told you that in case of something useful that you can contribute to me. So, for start this:

I'm looking out for apps or processes that I can use to gamify my current and future habits and tasks, in hope that I get better on doing things, first the necessary and basic.

Any recommendations or advice?
comment: I've found that gamifying can help, but it can also become another form of planning if you're not careful. For me, the bigger shift was tracking completed actions instead of designing the perfect system. A simple daily checklist or habit tracker worked better than a complex setup. Maybe start with the smallest version of reading/writing you can actually repeat for months.
